courses.title: Fire Protection Organization
courses.instructor: Neil A. Gamez, Staff02, Shane W. Warner
courses.description: FIRE 1 - Fire Protection Organization, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours This course provides an overview of fire protection and emergency services; career opportunities in fire protection and related fields; culture and history of emergency services; fire loss analysis; organization and function of public and private fire protection services; fire departments as part of local government; laws and regulations affecting the fire service; fire service nomenclature; specific fire protection functions; basic fire chemistry and physics; introduction to fire protection systems; introduction to fire strategy and tactics; life safety initiatives. Field trips may be required. Not repeatable. Transfer: (CSU) C-ID: (FIRE 100X)

courses.title: Spanish: Beginning II
courses.description: SPAN 2 - Spanish: Beginning II, 4 units Prerequisite(s): SPAN 1 or equivalent 72 Lecture Hours, 144 Out-of-Class Hours = 216 Total Student Learning Hours Continuation of SPAN1. This course continues to teach language acquisition in a historical and cultural context of the Spanish-speaking world through listening, speaking, reading, and writing at the second semester level. Students continue to interact using authentic language and will learn and communicate about the culture and civilizations of various countries in the Spanish-speaking world. A more in-depth knowledge of geography, famous sites, traditions, customs, literature, poetry, art, music, dance and history of Spanish-speaking countries will be covered. Students will continue to compare and contrast the nuances of everyday life in the Spanish-speaking world with those of English-speakers in the U.S. Equivalent to year three of high school Spanish. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (SPAN 102) Transfer: (CSU/UC) C-ID: (SPAN 110) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. For further clarification, see a counselor.

courses.title: History of California
courses.instructor: Theodore C. Rossier, Candice J. Hann
courses.description: HIST 11 - History of California, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours Survey of California history from pre-Colombian period to the present. Emphasis will include the Native American, Spaniards, Mexicans, and Anglo-Americans. Considerable attention will be devoted to California's influential role in national and world events. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (HIST 129) Transfer: (CSU/UC) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. courses.title: History of Graphic Design
courses.description: ART 15 - History of Graphic Design, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours This course is a survey of the evolution of Graphic Design beginning with the development of writing and ending with the revolution of digital media. It looks at the history of visual communication and how it has evolved in art, graphic design, illustration and popular culture from the 19th century to the latest trends and technologies. The survey takes into account sociopolitical and cultural contexts as well as artistic and technological characteristics of specific art movements. Students will engage in multiple in-depth discussions as well as create visual timelines to document the historical context, people, events, and creative work that have shaped the field of graphic design. Not repeatable. Transfer: (CSU/UC) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. For further clarification, see a counselor.

courses.title: World Civilizations: to 1500
courses.instructor: Peter T. Mueller, Shannon A. Dixon
courses.description: HIST 13 - World Civilizations: to 1500, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours Survey of the history of the world from the Neolithic period to the sixteenth century. The course will use a cross-cultural comparative approach as it analyzes the origins, achievements and decline of civilizations in Asia, Africa and the Americas, as well as the Middle East and Western Europe. Emphasis on the application of major theories of history to various stages of world development. The position of women in society will be highlighted. Not repeatable. Transfer: (CSU/UC) C-ID: (HIST 150) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. For further clarification, see a counselor.

courses.title: Introduction to Public Health
courses.description: HLTH 1 - Introduction to Public Health, 3 units Recommended for Success: ENGL C1000 or ENGL C1000E 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours This course provides a comprehensive introduction to the discipline of Public Health and applies public health theories to personal understanding of their community. Students will gain an in-depth understanding of the theoretical concepts and terminologies of public health, and the history and accomplishments of public health officials and agencies. Contrast quantitative and qualitative research methodologies in public health, analyzing how each approach shapes understanding of health issues, informs evidence-based decision-making, and applies to theory-driven research and preventative care strategies. An overview of the functions of various public health professions and institutions, and an in-depth examination of the core public health disciplines are covered. Topics of the discipline include the epidemiology of infectious and chronic disease; prevention and control of diseases in the community including the analysis of the social determinants of health and strategies for eliminating disease, illness and health disparities among various populations; community organizing and health promotion programming; environmental health and safety; global health; and healthcare policy and management. Not repeatable. Transfer: (CSU/UC) C-ID: (PH 101) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. For further clarification, see a counselor.

courses.title: Gender, Culture and Society
courses.description: SOCIO 7 - Gender, Culture and Society, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours The course takes an inclusive bio-cultural evolutionary perspective on gender, focusing on non-human primate societies as well as primitive (small scale) and modern (large scale) human societies. Factors such as culture, ecological conditions and historical circumstances, forces of stratification (e.g., age, social class), socialization (e.g., rites of passage, conformity and deviance) as well as the science (e.g. concepts, theories and methods) of studying these topics will be addressed. Though course readings will represent many disciplines, the foundation readings reflect the perspectives of bio-cultural anthropology as well as sociology. This emphasis addresses the fundamental assumption that while sex differences are biological, gender encompasses the traits that culture assigns and inculcates (with varying degrees of success) in males and females. Not repeatable. Transfer: (CSU/UC) C-ID: (SOCI 140) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. For further clarification, see a counselor.

courses.title: Introduction to Social Work and Human Services
courses.description: SWHS 1 - Introduction to Social Work and Human Services, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours An introductory overview of social welfare and the societal institutions in the U.S. that structure the provision of social services. This course examines social welfare within the context of cultural, economical, political, psychological and social forces. The course presents a historical perspective on the development of U.S. social work and human services. Special attention is given to current service delivery systems, their policies and procedures, and the tasks of culturally responsive social workers and human service workers within those settings. Knowledge, values and skills underpinning social work practice are introduced in this course. Not repeatable. Transfer: (CSU) C:ID: (SWHS 110)

courses.title: Computer Graphics
courses.description: ART 53/MEDIA 10 - Computer Graphics, 3 units 36 Lecture Hours, 54 Laboratory Hours, 72 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours Cross-listed with MEDIA10. This course introduces the student to the fundamentals of computer graphics. Topics include the elements and principles of design, concept development, characteristics of vector and raster digital files, color modes, digital drawing and painting, formatting for print and the web, and topics in AI (Artificial Intelligence) image generation. Students will acquire basic skills in current digital illustration software and create original design pieces. Credit may be earned for only one of the following: MEDIA10 or ART53.This course introduces the student to the fundamentals of computer graphics. courses.title: Career Exploration
courses.description: GUIDE 11 - Career Exploration, 1 unit 18 Lecture Hours, 36 Out-of-Class Hours = 54 Total Student Learning Hours An introduction to career exploration and career choice. Emphasis will be on linking personal information (interests, values and abilities) obtained through career assessment, with information about occupations, researched by using the Career Center and online resources. Career choices will be clarified and corresponding and appropriate educational goals will be selected. Students will receive instruction in goal setting, decision making, and problem solving as they relate to the development and fulfillment of educational and career plans. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (GUIDE 111) Transfer: (CSU/UC)

courses.title: Career/Life Planning
courses.instructor: Nicole Friend, Rosa N. Hernandez, Davia L. Shaffer
courses.description: GUIDE 1 - Career/Life Planning, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours This course presents methodologies in Career Counseling for students to develop an understanding of Career Development Theories and career educational planning. Students will critically evaluate their personality types based on self-awareness and objectivity in the areas of interests, skills, and values, using career theories. An introduction to decision-making, sources of occupational and career information, trends, and social influences on career-life planning will be developed. Students will develop a comprehensive educational and career plan. Integrating self-awareness, career exploration, and informed decision-making while recognizing the psychological, sociological, and physiological factors that influence career satisfaction and development across the lifespan. This course may include the administration of standardized interest and personality inventories. Not repeatable. Transfer: (CSU) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. For further clarification, see a counselor.

courses.description: HUMAN 1 - Old World Culture, 3 units Recommended for Success: ENGL C1000 or ENGL C1000E 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours This course examines the intellectual and artistic achievements of Western culture from prehistory to the Renaissance focusing on major movements, key figures, religious developments, and enduring works across art, architecture, literature, drama, philosophy, and music. Students will analyze and interpret these works within their historical, cultural, and philosophical contexts, compare and contrast diverse expressions of the human experience, and develop and advance historical understanding of major civilizations and cultures through the study of the ideas and artistic practices that have shaped Western civilization over time. courses.title: Introduction to Healthcare Careers
courses.description: HLTH 5 - Introduction to Healthcare Careers, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours This course provides an overview of healthcare careers and their respective career pathways, educational and skill requirements, and professional responsibilities. Basic skills required by health-related careers, such as emphasizing personal attributes, demonstrating professionalism, engaging in teamwork, and building communication skills will be covered. Students will be assisted in making educational and career decisions for a wide variety of health care occupations. Field trips may be required. Not repeatable. Transfer: (CSU)

sections.notes: Class takes place online anytime (with deadlines), but without scheduled live video conferences.

courses.title: Stress Management
courses.instructor: Tamara L. Oxford, Tina K. Trolier
courses.description: PSYCH 40 - Stress Management, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours An overview of the psychological, physiological, sociological and behavioral dynamics underlying the management of the human stress response. The class covers the biological and psychological aspects of the stress response, as well as the appraisal and management of stress. This includes time management, lifestyle choices, behavior modification techniques, relaxation training, and interpersonal communication techniques. Not repeatable. Transfer: (CSU) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. For further clarification, see a counselor.

courses.title: College Success
courses.instructor: Davia L. Shaffer, Vanessa Muro-Marai, Katie Marquez
courses.description: GUIDE 18 - College Success, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours This course equips students with strategies for thriving in higher education through a holistic approach to college success. Students will explore motivation, goal setting, educational planning, critical thinking, stress management, time management, and study skills. The course also covers personal wellness, interpersonal communication, cultural diversity, and college expectations. A key requirement is creating an educational plan to guide students in achieving their academic and personal goals. Not repeatable. Transfer: (CSU/UC) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. For further clarification, see a counselor.

courses.title: Introduction to Philosophy
courses.description: PHILO 1 - Introduction to Philosophy, 3 units Recommended for Success: ENGL C1000 or ENGL C1000E 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours A careful and critical examination of some of the Great Questions philosophers have pursued from ancient times to the present concerning the nature of reality, knowledge, and value. Some of these include: What is real? What is human nature? Do we have free will? Does God exist? What can we know? How should we act? What is the ideal community? And, what is the nature of truth? Course also includes how to think philosophically. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (PHILO 101) Transfer:(CSU/UC) C-ID: (PHIL 100) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. For further clarification, see a counselor.

courses.title: Spanish: Beginning I
courses.description: SPAN 1 - Spanish: Beginning I, 4 units 72 Lecture Hours, 144 Out-of-Class Hours = 216 Total Student Learning Hours This introductory course teaches beginning language development in a cultural context through listening, speaking, reading, and writing. Students will interact using authentic basic language at the beginning level as well as learn and communicate about the culture and civilization of various countries in the Spanish-speaking world. Equivalent to two years of high school Spanish. MJC equivalent: (SPAN 101) Transfer: (CSU/UC) C-ID: (SPAN 100) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. courses.title: World Regional Geography
courses.description: GEOGR 20 - World Regional Geography, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours Survey of the world's culture regions and nations as interpreted by geographers, including physical, cultural, and economic features. Emphasis on spatial and historical influences on population growth, transportation networks, and natural environments. Identification and importance of the significant features of regions. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (GEOG 110) Transfer: (CSU/UC) C-ID: (GEOG 125) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. For further clarification, see a counselor.

courses.title: Health and Fitness Education
courses.instructor: Sara A. Mitchell, Nathan C. Rien
courses.description: HHP 60 - Health and Fitness Education, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours This course will require students to explore, analyze, personalize, and discuss the following issues as they relate to the essential components of health and wellness: nutrition, physical activity/exercise/fitness, weight control, eating disorders and body image, media influences, mental health, stress, violence, substance use/abuse, sexuality and sexual orientation, sexually transmitted infections, reproductive choices/contraception, relationships, disease prevention, environment, health care, aging, and general public health issues. Students will be taught the knowledge and skills necessary to implement lifestyle behaviors that can improve their health and well-being. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (HE 110) Transfer: (CSU/UC) C-ID: (PH 100)

courses.title: Nutrition
courses.description: BIOL 50 - Nutrition, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours Introductory study of energy and nutrient requirements of the body in relation to growth, maintenance, and reproduction; factors influencing normal metabolism, construction of the adequate diet. Emphasis is placed upon the chemical aspects of nutrition. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (FDNTR 219) Transfer: (CSU/UC) C-ID: (NUTR 110) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. courses.title: Infant/Toddler Care and Education
courses.description: CHILD 43 - Infant/Toddler Care and Education, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours This course applies current theory and research to the care and education of infants and toddlers in group settings. Students will examine essential policies, principles and practices that lead to quality care and developmentally appropriate curriculum for children birth to 36 months. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (CLDDV 122) Transfer: (CSU)

courses.title: Administration I: Programs in Early Childhood Education
courses.description: CHILD 30 - Administration I: Programs in Early Childhood Education, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours This course is an introduction to the administration of early childhood programs. Covers program types, budget, management, regulations, laws, development and implementation of policies and procedures. Examines administrative tools, philosophies, and techniques needed to organize, open, and operate an early care and education program that is responsive to the needs of the community. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (CLDDV 150) Transfer: (CSU)

courses.title: Admin II: Personnel & Leadership in Early Childhood Education
courses.description: CHILD 31 - Admin II: Personnel & Leadership in Early Childhood Education 3 Units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours This course will examine effective strategies for personnel management and leadership in early care and education settings. Includes legal and ethical responsibilities, supervision techniques, professional development, and reflective practices for a diverse and inclusive early care and education program. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (CLDDV 151) Transfer: (CSU)

courses.title: Child Growth and Development
courses.instructor: Donna V. Martin, Kristina M. Miro, Not Listed
courses.description: CHILD 1 - Child Growth and Development, 3 units 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours Examines the progression of development in the physical, cognitive, social, and emotional domains and identifies developmental milestones for children from conception through adolescence. Emphasis on interactions between biological processes and environmental factors. Students will observe children, evaluate individual differences, and analyze characteristics of development at various stages according to developmental theories. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (CLDDV 103) Transfer: (CSU/UC) C-ID: (CDEV 100) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. For further clarification, see a counselor.

courses.title: American Literature: Colonial Period - Late 19th Century
courses.description: ENGL 17 - American Literature: Colonial Period - Late 19th Century, 3 units Prerequisite(s): ENGL C1000 or ENGL C1000E Recommended for Success: ENGL C1002 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours A study of American literature from its beginning to the late nineteenth century. Reading, analysis, and discussion of the major literary trends and authors of the time, including Emerson, Thoreau, Poe, Hawthorne, Melville, Whitman, and Dickinson. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (ENGL 135) Transfer: (CSU/UC) C-ID: (ENGL 130) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. courses.description: ECON C2002 - Principles of Macroeconomics, 3 units Formerly listed as: ECON 10 Prerequisite: Prerequisite: Placement as determined by the college's multiple measures assessment process or completion of a course taught at or above the level of elementary algebra. 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours An introductory course using models of the domestic and international economy to understand national income, unemployment, inflation, economic growth, inequality, the financial system, and monetary, fiscal, and other economic policies. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (ECON C2002) Transfer: (CSU/UC) C-ID: (ECON 202) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. courses.title: Lifespan Human Development
courses.description: PSYCH 10 - Lifespan Human Development, 3 units Recommended for Success: PSYC C1000 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours An introduction to the scientific study of the human being from conception to death. The interplay of biological, psychological, social, and cultural forces on the developing human will be examined. As well as examining universal development, the course will examine individual differences in human development that include developmental problems associated with physical, cognitive, social, and personality issues. Instruction will include theoretical concepts as well as practical applications. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (PSYCH 141) Transfer: (CSU/UC) C-ID: (PSY 180) General Education: Using catalog rights, check catalog for course general education approvals. For further clarification, see a counselor.

Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (CLDDV 125) Transfer: (CSU)

courses.title: Job Search and Interviewing Strategies
courses.description: GUIDE 25 - Job Search and Interviewing Strategies, 1 unit 18 Lecture Hours, 36 Out-of-Class Hours = 54 Total Student Learning Hours Understanding the employment process and development of written and oral presentation skills necessary to conduct an efficient and effective job search. Topics include: the hiring process, employer perspectives, the hidden job market, networking, research, job search planning, making employer contacts and interviewing. Development of a master application, resume and letter of application. Not repeatable. MJC equivalent: (GUIDE 112) Transfer: (CSU)

courses.title: Photo Editing for Digital and Print Publication
courses.description: MEDIA 12 - Photo Editing for Digital and Print Publication, 3 units 36 Lecture Hours, 54 Laboratory Hours, 72 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours This course focuses on the principles and practices of photo editing, artistic expression, and development of problem-solving skills, using an industry standard photo editing software program. The tools and techniques used to create effective and sophisticated digital imagery for websites, multimedia and print publications are surveyed. AI (Artificial Intelligence) generative imagery will be examined and utilized. Not repeatable. Transfer: (CSU)

courses.title: Building Construction for Fire Protection
courses.description: FIRE 4 - Building Construction for Fire Protection, 3 units Prerequisite(s): FIRE 1 54 Lecture Hours, 108 Out-of-Class Hours = 162 Total Student Learning Hours This course provides the components of building construction related to firefighter and life safety with an emphasis on firefighter safety on new construction. The elements of construction and design of structures are shown to be key factors when inspecting buildings, preplanning fire operations, and operating at an emergency scene. The development and evolution of building and fire codes will be studied in relation to past fires in residential, commercial, and industrial occupancies. Field trips may be required. Not repeatable. Transfer: (CSU) C-ID: (FIRE 130X)

courses.instructor: Erica N. Krumeich, Davia L. Shaffer, Staff03, Rosa N. Hernandez
courses.description: GUIDE 35 - Basics of Budgeting and Money Management, 1 unit 18 Lecture Hours, 36 Out-of-Class Hours = 54 Total Student Learning Hours This course provides students with the fundamental tools to make informed decisions that impact their short and long-term finances. Topics covered include money management and the decision processes and behaviors underlying spending, saving, and borrowing. Not repeatable. Transfer: (CSU)
